name: Mary's Honey-Walnut Swirl
recipeInstructions:
Honey Nut Filling
3/4 cup sugar
1/4 cup honey
1 egg -- beaten
1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract
1 teaspoon ground cinnamon
1/4 teaspoon salt
1/2 cup walnuts -- chopped
Powdered Sugar Glaze
1 1/2 cups powdered sugar -- sifted
1/2 tablespoon butter -- melted
2 tablespoons hot water
1. In a bowl, combine yeast with 1 cup of water. When the yeast starts to bubble, add eggs, 1 cup of water, oil, orange peel, and lemon peel. Blend well.
2. Add Hot Roll Mix one cup at a time to make a soft dough. On a floured surface, knead dough for 5 to 7 minutes until smooth and satiny.
3. Turn the dough out onto a buttered bowl and turn to coat with butter. Cover with a damp towel and let rise in a warm place until doubled, about 1 hour.
4. Punch down the dough. Prepare Honey-Nut Filling by buttering two 9"x5 inches loaf pans.
5. Divide the dough into half. Roll out each half to 9"x14", about 1/2 inch thick.
6. Spread Honey-Nut Filling over the center of the dough, leaving about 1 inch from the edges. Roll up from the small end, lifting the dough slightly and sealing the edges as you roll. Seal the ends and place into prepared pans, seam side down.
7. Cover and let rise in a warm place until the dough is slightly rounded above the top of the pan.
8. Preheat oven to 375°F. Bake for 45 to 50 minutes, or until golden brown. Cool.
9. Drizzle Powdered-Sugar Glaze over warm loaves.
Honey-Nut Filling
Combine all ingredients in a small bowl and blend well.
Powdered-Sugar Glaze
Combine all ingredients in a small bowl and blend well.
